To generate synthetic data to address data imbalance, you can use various techniques such as SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique), ADASYN (Adaptive Synthetic Sampling), or random oversampling. Here, I will provide a Python solution using the SMOTE technique from the `imbalanced-learn` library.

First, you need to install the required libraries:

```bash
pip install pandas numpy imbalanced-learn
```

Then, you can use the following Python code:

```python
import pandas as pd
from imblearn.over_sampling import SMOTE
import numpy as np

# Load your dataset
data = {
    "LIMIT_BAL": [320000, 50000, 400000, 350000, 40000, 50000, 160000, 30000, 310000, 80000, 200000, 160000, 140000, 50000, 20000, 460000, 60000, 30000, 50000, 50000, 50000, 200000, 10000, 30000, 50000, 80000, 10000, 200000, 80000, 100000, 600000, 160000, 60000, 50000, 50000, 160000, 120000, 30000, 50000, 50000, 50000, 710000, 40000, 440000, 50000, 170000, 220000, 340000, 90000, 160000, 50000, 50000, 500000, 50000, 30000, 210000, 30000, 160000, 60000, 160000, 110000, 30000, 300000, 30000, 50000, 500000, 80000, 50000, 50000, 500000, 80000, 260000, 20000, 50000, 60000, 50000, 470000, 150000, 300000, 200000, 100000, 330000, 20000, 150000, 250000, 30000, 200000, 70000, 130000, 70000, 170000, 210000, 300000, 30000, 200000, 10000, 120000, 20000, 220000, 80000, 30000, 300000, 150000, 300000, 360000, 180000, 500000, 50000, 170000, 220000, 340000, 90000, 140000, 500000, 50000, 30000, 210000, 30000, 160000, 60000, 160000, 110000, 30000, 300000, 30000, 50000, 500000, 80000, 50000, 50000, 500000, 80000, 260000, 20000, 50000, 60000, 50000, 470000, 150000, 300000, 200000, 100000, 330000, 20000, 150000, 250000, 30000, 200000, 70000, 130000, 70000, 170000, 210000, 300000, 30000, 200000,
